_comment: Tier-1 keyword shortcuts for the cookbook-chapter classifier. Title-only, word-boundary phrase matching. Each entry is a SPECIFIC dish noun whose chapter is unambiguous regardless of ingredients or context. NEVER add bare protein words (chicken/beef/etc.), broad chapter words (salad/pie/cake bare), sauce names that travel in dish titles (pesto/curry), cross-locale homonyms (biscuit/pudding/tortilla), or substring hazards (tart/pie/cake bare). The __DEFER__ sentinel marks known trap phrases that contain a shortcut keyword but actually shouldn't shortcut — they force the LLM. Edit this file to add/remove entries; the classifier validates every value against CHAPTERS at load time and fails loudly on mismatch.
_sauce_names_note: Sauce names removed from Tier-1 — they travel in dish titles: 'Celery Salad with Green Apple Vinaigrette' is a SALAD, not a sauce. 'Chimichurri Steak' is meat, not a sauce. 'Asparagus with Hollandaise' is a vegetable. The LLM handles these correctly (a standalone 'Vinaigrette' recipe routes to Sauces; a salad recipe with vinaigrette in the title routes to Salads). Cost of one LLM call per actual standalone sauce recipe: ~$0.0001. Worth it.
